data.title: Security company CertiK is considering an IPO with a target valuation of $2 billion.
data.description: Blockchain security auditing firm CertiK is exploring the possibility of an initial public offering (IPO), targeting a valuation of approximately $2 billion. Ronghui Gu, co-founder of CertiK and associate professor of computer science at Columbia University, stated in an interview this week in Davos, Switzerland, that while a specific listing timeline has not yet been established, an IPO has become a clear goal for the company. Founded in 2018 and headquartered in New York, CertiK primarily provides smart contract auditing and security services for blockchain projects. Its most recent funding round occurred in 2022, when it raised $88 million in a Series B3 funding round led by Insight Partners, Tiger Global, and Advent International, valuing the company at $2 billion. Ronghui Gu stated that Binance is CertiK's earliest and currently largest financial backer, and its investors also include Coinbase and SoftBank.

data.title: Nasdaq issues another delisting notice to Bitcoin treasury company K Wave Media
data.description: Nasdaq has once again issued a delisting notice to K Wave Media, the K-Pop media company employing a Bitcoin vault strategy. This time, the company's market capitalization (MVLS) failed to meet Nasdaq's minimum market capitalization requirement. Nasdaq requires K Wave Media to maintain a market capitalization of $50 million or more for 10 consecutive trading days by June 2026, or it will be unable to continue listing on the Nasdaq Global Market. Previously, K Wave Media secured $1 billion in capital to support its Bitcoin vault strategy. This year, it received a delisting notice from Nasdaq after its stock price remained below $1 for 30 consecutive trading days, requiring the company to raise its stock price to above $1 by July 6, 2026, and maintain that level for 10 consecutive trading days to avoid delisting.

data.title: A trader with a rollover position had their short positions liquidated this morning, worth approximately $60 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, at 1:00 AM today, BTC broke through $91,000 and ETH broke through $3,000, causing a short position held by a trader to hit the liquidation price, liquidating a position worth approximately $60 million. The trader's short position value decreased from $300 million to $238 million, and unrealized profit decreased from a high of $24 million to less than $4 million.

data.title: UK FCA Seeks Feedback on Crypto Consumer Rules
data.description:
The UK FCA enters the final stage of crypto regulations, inviting feedback on consumer rules.
source: https://coincu.com/news/uk-fca-crypto-consumer-rules/

data.title: Stable Blockchain to Upgrade Mainnet to Version 1.2.0 in February
data.description: Stable, the Tether-backed Layer 1 blockchain, announced that its mainnet will be upgraded to version 1.2.0 on February 4. According to PANews, this update will switch the native gas to USDT0 and eliminate the existing wrap/unwrap process. Additionally, the upgrade aims to enhance the observability of the indexer's staking lifecycle and improve compatibility for developers across the network.

data.title: Stablecoins transferred over $35 trillion last year, but only 1% of that went to real-world payments.
data.description: A new report from consulting firm McKinsey and blockchain data company Artemis Analytics shows that stablecoins transferred over $35 trillion on the blockchain last year, but only about 1% of that was for real-world payments. The analysis estimates that only $380 billion in activity reflected actual payments, such as payments to suppliers, remittances, or payroll. This represents approximately 0.02% of total global payments, while McKinsey estimates that total global payments exceed $2 trillion annually. (Coindesk)

data.title: The BiHelix ecosystem token MCU616, a native Bitcoin infrastructure, is about to be listed on the XT exchange.
data.description: According to official sources, MCU616, a token built on the BiHelix ecosystem, the native Bitcoin infrastructure, will soon be listed on the XT exchange, enabling spot trading. MCU616 is developed based on the Bitcoin RGB protocol, with "Marvel Multiverse" as its ecosystem theme. It integrates meme culture and application vision, aiming to build multi-asset narratives and community-driven application exploration on top of Bitcoin.

data.description:

data.title: Litecoin Structure Intact, But $63 Remains The Line Bulls Must Defend
data.description:
Litecoin is once again at a critical crossroads, with its long-term structure remaining intact after years of successful defenses. However, the margin for error is thin. As price hovers near key levels, $63 has emerged as the line bulls must protect. A break below it could shift momentum sharply, while holding above keeps the broader bullish structure alive and sets the stage for the next decisive move
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/litecoin/litecoin-structure-intact/

data.description:
The massive increase in the number of active addresses and on-chain transactions was due to the mass distribution of poisoning dust.
source: https://ambcrypto.com/ethereum-hits-1-29m-active-addresses-but-security-risks-loom-why/

data.title: Lombard Finance's $BARD Gains Traction Post-TGE
data.description: Stacy Muur posted on X that Lombard Finance's $BARD is among the few altcoins she is currently staking to accumulate more points. The cryptocurrency has shown promising traction following its Token Generation Event (TGE), with consistent performance noted by investors.

data.title: The FBI arrested a former Olympic skier suspected of using cryptocurrency to transfer drug proceeds.
data.description: Former Olympic snowboarder Ryan Wedding, accused by U.S. authorities of using cryptocurrency for drug trafficking, has been arrested in Mexico and extradited to the United States. U.S. authorities arrested the former Canadian snowboarder on Friday, along with a drug lord suspected of using cryptocurrency networks to transfer and launder the proceeds of his crimes. Wedding was previously listed on the FBI's Ten Most Wanted Fugitives list for alleged cocaine trafficking and murder. According to the FBI, Wedding had links to the Sinaloa drug cartel and was sanctioned by the U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control in November. (Coindesk)

data.title: Nina Rong: We are currently verifying details of the BNB Chain Coinmarketcap account being hacked.
data.description: Nina Rong, Executive Director of Growth at BNB Chain, stated that she has received reports of a BNB Chain Coinmarketcap account being hacked and is currently verifying the details with her security and internal audit teams. She also cautioned against making any investment decisions posted on social media.

data.title: Ethereum Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: Ethereum's price fluctuations could lead to substantial liquidations on major centralized exchanges. According to ChainCatcher, Coinglass data indicates that if Ethereum's price falls below $2,805, the cumulative liquidation intensity of long positions on these exchanges could reach $837 million. Conversely, if Ethereum surpasses $3,100, the cumulative liquidation intensity of short positions could amount to $723 million.

data.title: Gemini Earn saga ends as SEC backs off
data.description:
The US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) took a major step in the lawsuit against Gemini Trust Company over the failed Gemini Earn program. The watchdog dropped the case against the company. This move ended one of the most closely watched crypto enforcement cases, which emerged from the 2022 market collapse. In a joint filing […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/gemini-earn-saga-ends-as-sec-backs-off/

data.title: US stocks closed mixed in the crypto sector, with ETHZ up 0.76% and VIX up 0.55%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.58%, the S&P 500 rose 0.03%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.28%. The crypto sector saw more declines than gains, with ETHZ closing up 0.76% and TRON up 0.63%; VIX closed down 0.55%, BMNR down 0.11%, and CRCL down 0.34%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: US lawmakers have submitted several amendments to the cryptocurrency market structure bill, including a ban on the president and other individuals trading digital assets.
data.description: Ahead of next week's Senate Agriculture Committee hearings, debates, and votes on the cryptocurrency market structure bill, Democratic lawmakers have submitted several proposed amendments. One amendment aims to add a "Digital Asset Ethics Act" to the bill. This amendment would prohibit "regulated personnel," such as the president, vice president, and members of Congress, from engaging in certain financial transactions involving digital assets. Bloomberg has estimated that Trump has profited approximately $1.4 billion from his cryptocurrency investments, including investments from the DeFi and stablecoin project World Liberty Financial. The Trump family also holds a 20% stake in the mining company American Bitcoin. Other amendments include measures to prevent fraudulent transactions through "digital asset self-service terminals" and a requirement to postpone the enactment of future cryptocurrency legislation until at least four commissioners are appointed to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). This issue has been a point of contention among some lawmakers, as the CFTC currently has only one commissioner, while the maximum number of commissioners is five. (The Block)
